programme.title: Meaningful Beauty
programme.desc: Feeling beautiful can give women self-confidence at every age. That? s the foundation of Meaningful Beauty. Achieving healthy, glowing skin is a reflection of taking good care of yourself and accepting who you are. After that, you can go enjoy your life.

programme.title: Anne Frank, Entebbe, Sacrifice
programme.desc: Unpacking the history of Anne Frank and how she became an icon, the 1976 Raid on Entebbe in Uganda, and the Power of Sacrifice.

programme.title: Ze'ev Jabotinsky, Peace, Happiness
programme.desc: Unpacking the history of the 20th century right-wing Zionist Ze'ev Jabotinsky, the challenging path to peace in the Middle East, and the Power of Happiness.

programme.title: A New York City Shabbat with Neshama Carlebach
programme.desc: Jewish music legend Neshama Carlebach leads a special, uplifting Shabbat from the Bitter End in New York City's Greenwich Village.

programme.title: Wildest Places S03 E04 - Coast
programme.desc: Australia has more than 35,000 kilometers of coastline. From broad, sandy beaches to towering cliffs and mangrove swamps, Australia’s coast can be a strange mix of soil and sea; rainforest and reef.

programme.title: Wildest Places S01 E01 - Bays
programme.desc: The calm azure waters of the Coral Triangle are fringed with jungle, coconut trees and mangroves. Centuries of seismic activity have lined these bays with volcanic ash and mud. Rather than smothering life, these ‘muck zones’ have borne some of the marine world’s most bizarre adaptations.

programme.title: Wildest Places S01 E02 - Reefs
programme.desc: This episode looks at the various habitats and areas of a coral reef, and how each zone within this extremely complex and delicately balanced ecosystem houses life forms that adapt in unique ways.

programme.title: Art & The City S01 E05 - London
programme.desc: David Keeps explores London’s vibrant art scene from edgy Camberwell hotels and Tate Modern bridges to royal relics and Hoxton galleries uncovering creative contrasts in a city where ancient tradition and modern expression collide with wit.

programme.title: Art & The City S01 E06 - Los Angeles
programme.desc: David Keeps explores Los Angeles’s creative energy from cutting edge Culver City galleries and Disney Hall to the Getty’s bold exhibits and Chinatown’s emerging scene revealing how art, architecture, and personality fuel this sprawling cultural capital.

programme.title: American Canvas S01 E03 - Miami
programme.desc: Dave Holmes explores Miami’s dynamic cultural scene, from the international art spectacle of Art Basel to Little Havana’s rich culinary traditions, Wynwood’s street art, quickie couture fashion, flamenco dance, and dazzling Art Deco architecture.

programme.title: Euromaxx Architecture E65 - Bright Ideas, Bold Urban Forms
programme.desc: Living With Wood The Courage to Use Color Europe’s Highest Steeple The Twist Sculpture Gallery A City Made of Cardboard Berlin's Studio karhard

programme.title: Euromaxx Architecture E66 - Sustainable Living, Timeless Design Revival
programme.desc: Eighteen Stories and Made of Wood Bauhaus-style Architecture in Pamplona Pimp My Caravan Sustainable Building: the Cork House An Island Home in Brittany Frankfurt's New Old Town

programme.title: Race Across The World S03 E04 - Episode 4 NB
programme.desc: ‘Race Across the World’ is back, as five teams attempt to race sixteen thousand kilometres through the second largest country in the world – Canada. Without smartphones, internet access and credit cards, and armed only with the cash equivalent of the airfare to fly the route, our teams travel this extraordinary journey at ground level. Their skill, ingenuity and determination will be tested to the extreme as they race through some of the world’s most remote regions, where they must also rely on the kindness of strangers in order to stand the best chance of reaching the finish line. This series takes viewers on the trip of a lifetime, following our racers as they embark on an epic and adrenaline-fueled adventure, traveling into the unknown without the trappings of modern life.

programme.title: Euromaxx On Vacation E80 - Alpine Roads, Russian Beauty, Belgian Grit
programme.desc: Through Mountains and Fjords: Norway’s Flåmsbana Belgium’s first female horseback shrimper Karelia: An Unspoiled Beauty Spot in Russia Alpine Circle - Exploring the Swiss Alps on an E-Bike Madeira: Remote working in vacationland The “Water Doctor”: Kneipp Turns 200

programme.title: Race Across The World S03 E05 - Episode 5 NB
programme.desc: The BAFTA-award winning third series of Race Across the World has reached the half-way point and our four teams are one leg closer to the £20,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Euromaxx On Vacation E83 - La Rambla, Tulips, And Skywalks
programme.desc: Harry Potter and the Auld Reekie Blossoming Tulips in The Netherlands The Most Famous Street in Barcelona: La Rambla Via Ferrata: 600 Meters Above the Abyss Glamping: Nights Spent Under Starry Skies.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay’s Great Escape S01 E02 - India Part 2
programme.desc: Gordon’s got a taste for adventure and heads off the tourist track into impenetrable north-east India, home of the country’s best-kept culinary secrets. Leaving the 21st century behind, he immerses himself in the life of a local meat-loving tribe in Nagaland, once better known for head-hunting than their curries. Then he’s off to Assam to enter a housewives’ competition to find the best Assamese dish; and finally he sets up shop among the street food stalls of Calcutta to try and tempt the local foodies into buying north-east curry – Ramsay style.

programme.title: Paradise Kitchen Bali S01 E09 Mushroom
programme.desc: Indulge in Lauren's exploration of the chocolate world as she uncovers its unique types and characteristics. From savory chocolate salsa to raw vegan cake, her mix of sweet and savory recipes leaves a lasting appreciation for this beloved ingredient.

programme.title: Paradise Kitchen Bali S01 E10 Chocolate
programme.desc: Lauren dives into the fascinating world of mushrooms, exploring their flavors, textures, and health benefits. She shows how to make savory mushroom sausage rolls and delicious mushroom steaks, showcasing the versatility of these earthy ingredients.

programme.title: Euromaxx À La Carte E119 - Belgian Waffles, Sandwich, Yogurt, Kumpir
programme.desc: Thin and crispy: Alsatian tarte flambée. Kumpir: the secrets of Turkey’s baked potato. The Magic of Belgian Waffles. The Sandwich – Simple and Good. Bulgarian Yogurt: A Fountain of youth?

programme.title: Euromaxx À La Carte E121 - Chocolate Lover’s Dream, Stilton, Pizza
programme.desc: Kalter Hund – A chocolate lover's dream! Cheesy comestibles: Stilton from England. Round, Colorful and Sugary Sweet: Macarons. Pizza: Italy’s Classic Street Food. The Taste of Albania: A 24-hour Food Tour of Tirana.

programme.title: Lucky In Love S01 E02 - Gary and Dean in Washington D.C. NB
programme.desc: Gary and Dean explore the capital city by the toss of a coin. Their journey includes activities ranging from kayaking on the Potomac River to trying the city's best chili dogs.

programme.title: Lucky In Love S01 E03 - Rachel and Paul in New Orleans NB
programme.desc: Rachel and Paul explore The Big Easy by letting a coin toss decide their path, creating a unique way to discover the city's culture and attractions

programme.title: Beach Battles S01 E01 - Key West Wrightsville
programme.desc: Dive into the fun, blowing conchs in Key West, Florida and race pro SUPS in Wrightsville Beach, North Carolina.

programme.title: Americanos E01 - Austin, Texas
programme.desc: We explore Austin's vibrant culture through the lens of Latinx influences, focusing on its evolution from a Wild West town to a creative, tech-focused hub. In this episode, viewers explore the world of Tejanos, from Selena fandom and vaqueros (the original cowboys of Texas) to taco joints, barbeque, and the legendary independent film and music scene that spawned Austin City Limits and SXSW.

programme.title: Americanos E02 - Los Angeles, California NB
programme.desc: From Compton to Highland Park, this episode traverses Los Angeles to meet three Americanos who are carving out a place for themselves within film, food, and beauty. Distinctly drawing on their diverse backgrounds and deeply influenced by the city they live in, audiences get a better sense of how Latinx culture has permeated the streets, runway, and silver screen.

programme.title: Into Americas Wild NB
programme.desc: Narrated by Morgan Freeman, this documentary short takes viewers on a scenic, fast-paced journey through the United States' natural wonders. The film emphasizes the connection between humans and nature, exploring locations from the Alaskan wilderness to the Appalachian Trail and Utah's canyons.

programme.title: Trails & Trailblazers S01 E01 - Alaska NB
Trails & Trailblazers S01 E02 - Oregon NB
Trails & Trailblazers S01 E03 - Arizona NB
Travel Man S02 E01 - Vienna
programme.desc: Richard goes to Vienna with comedian Chris O'Dowd to sample the sausages, cakes, wines and spirits. The pair also take a hot-rod tour, visit a fairground, the Freud museum and the city sewers.

programme.title: Travel Man S02 E02 - Paris
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade and Mel Giedroyc do a Paris le weekend, with snails, absinthe, a cordon bleu cookery course, bespoke perfume and an art tour.

programme.title: Travel Man S02 E03 - Copenhagen
programme.desc: Comedian Noel Fielding joins Richard for 48 fast and funny hours in the Danish capital, with cycling, sightseeing, art, sandwiches, beer and rollercoasters.

programme.title: Travel Man S02 E04 - Moscow
programme.desc: Richard and Greg Davies attempt to extract the essence of Moscow in two days, as they clash with army tanks, head into space and visit one of the strangest circuses in the world.

programme.title: Epic Trails S01 E05 - Peru
programme.desc: Eric Hanson explores the amazing culture, hikes, and ruins of Peru's Sacred Valley.

programme.title: Epic Trails S01 E06 - Winter in Banff and Lake Louise
programme.desc: Eric returns to Banff National Park and Lake Louise in the snowy months to explore the backcountry.

programme.title: Chasing The Sun S02 E05 - Malaysia
programme.desc: Where else can you find a beach full of cats after shopping in a vintage vinyl store in the heart of the city or watch a shadow puppet show after having a home-cooked meal prepared by two entrepreneurs?

programme.title: Rock The Park S06 E21 - Petrified Forest National Park
programme.desc: Jack and Colton are in Arizona's Petrified Forest National Park checking out one of the world’s most highly concentrated areas of petrified wood.

programme.title: Rock The Park S06 E22 - Los Angeles - From Surf To Snow
programme.desc: Colton and Jack take a well-known Los Angeles challenge… surfing and skiing in one day! Plus, they experience the power of youth spending time in nature as they surf with new friends from Girl Powerful!

programme.title: Euromaxx On Vacation E81 - Northern Spain To Saxony’s Castles
programme.desc: Saxony’s Most Beautiful Castles E-biking Through Istria The Secrets of the Ponte Vecchio in Florence The Highlights of Bilbao, Northern Spain Discoveries in the North German Wadden Sea.

programme.title: Euromaxx On Vacation E82 - Sofia’s Past To Iceland’s Peaks
programme.desc: Europe by Train: A Ride on the Glacier Express Finding Our Way Around Amsterdam Through the Ages in Sofia A Tip for Nature Fans: Iceland’s West Fjords Europe’s Longest Place Name The Renaissance Festival in Tortosa

programme.title: Euromaxx On Vacation E85 - Madrid, Venice, Vienna & Latvia Vibes
programme.desc: How to Run a Sustainable Hotel Venice on Instagram and Up-Close Latvian Midsummer Night: the Līgo Festival Travel Tips: How to Spend the Perfect Day in Madrid. Delving into Empress Sisi's Legacy in Vienna"

programme.title: Euromaxx At Its Best E07 - Scandinavia At Its Best
programme.desc: Admire the fjords of Norway on a boat trip, find out whether Copenhagen is really as cool as Instagram claims and discover the charm of Helsinki's modern architecture.

programme.title: Euromaxx At Its Best E09 - The Baltic At Its Best
programme.desc: Discover the three Baltic states of Lithuania, Latvia and Estonia with us. Stroll through the streets of Vilnius, Riga and Tallinn. Savour bizarre ice cream creations. Marvel at the art of an Estonian acrobat and relax in a forest house where the blues are always playing.

programme.title: Euromaxx At Its Best E10 - Germany At Its Best
programme.desc: See unique Berlin attractions, enjoy the Lech river, visit the eco-friendly city Freiburg and finish with a Bavarian Schweinshaxe.

programme.title: Andiamo! UPTOWN S01 E05 - Napa Valley, California
programme.desc: Donna visits California's Napa Valley where she will experience some of the great wines of the region and some of its unique offerings.

programme.title: Andiamo! UPTOWN S01 E06 - Central Thailand
programme.desc: Donna travels to Thailand for a 3-part production of "andiamo!". In this episode, Bangkok and Hua Hin are highlighted with a great deal of culture, cuisine, a Muay Thai boxing match and more!

programme.title: Andiamo! UPTOWN S01 E06 - Central Thailand
programme.desc: Donna travels to Thailand for a 3-part production of "andiamo!". In this episode, Bangkok and Hua Hin are highlighted with a great deal of culture, cuisine, a Muay Thai boxing match and more!

programme.title: The RVers S01 E04 - Choosing An RV - Towables
programme.desc: Anthony learns about towables, both massive and tiny.

programme.title: The RVers S01 E05 - Leveling An RV
programme.desc: John shows us why not having a level RV can melt the ice cream in your freezer.

programme.title: Epic Trails S01 E05 - Peru
programme.desc: Eric Hanson explores the amazing culture, hikes, and ruins of Peru's Sacred Valley.

programme.title: Epic Trails S01 E06 - Winter in Banff and Lake Louise
programme.desc: Eric returns to Banff National Park and Lake Louise in the snowy months to explore the backcountry.

programme.title: Rock The Park S06 E21 - Petrified Forest National Park
programme.desc: Jack and Colton are in Arizona's Petrified Forest National Park checking out one of the world’s most highly concentrated areas of petrified wood.

programme.title: Rock The Park S06 E22 - Los Angeles - From Surf To Snow
programme.desc: Colton and Jack take a well-known Los Angeles challenge… surfing and skiing in one day! Plus, they experience the power of youth spending time in nature as they surf with new friends from Girl Powerful!

programme.title: Chasing The Sun S02 E05 - Malaysia
programme.desc: Where else can you find a beach full of cats after shopping in a vintage vinyl store in the heart of the city or watch a shadow puppet show after having a home-cooked meal prepared by two entrepreneurs?

programme.title: On the Record with Mick Rock S01 E01 - Josh Groban in Los Angeles NB
programme.desc: Native Angeleno and multi-platinum recording artist Josh Groban takes Mick on an inspiring tour of Los Angeles, paying homage to the high school teacher who gave him the confidence to succeed, stopping by the after-school Shakespeare program he personally supports and treating Mick to a moving performance of his song, “Hidden Away.”

programme.title: On the Record with Mick Rock S01 E02 - Kings of Leon in Nashville NB
programme.desc: Mick discovers how the Grammy Award-winning rock stars, Kings of Leon, escaped their Pentecostal roots to find a home in Nashville’s evolving music scene. The band dishes on its rocky past and gives Mick a tour of country music’s hometown, ending the day in the Kings’ private studio for an exclusive jam session.

programme.title: On the Record with Mick Rock S01 E03 - The Flaming Lips in Oklahoma City NB
programme.desc: Mick touches down in Oklahoma City to hang with the Flaming Lips and uncovers how these masters of psychedelic rock have become the surprising hometown heroes. Lead singer Wayne Coyne shows off his wacky art gallery, fills Mick in on his all-night parties with Miley Cyrus and ends the day with a performance of the group’s hit song, “Do You Realize?”

programme.title: On the Record with Mick Rock S01 E04 - Patti Labelle in Philadelphia NB
programme.desc: Grammy Hall of Fame artist Patti LaBelle takes Mick on a personal tour of Philadelphia, visiting the theater that launched her career and stopping by her childhood home where she first found her voice with a broomstick microphone. After grabbing a bite at her favorite soul food spot in the City of Brotherly Love, Patti invites Mick into her living room for an intimate performance of What Can I Do For You?

programme.title: HEALTH EXPLORED S02 E01 Friluftsliv (Part 1)
programme.desc: We travel to Tromso to learn about the concept of “Open Air Life” from the inhabitants of this arctic region. Feed reindeer and take in the mountains with a Saami guide.

programme.title: Health Explored S02 E02 - Friluftsliv Pt 2
programme.desc: We go sea to summit with a skiing experience. We trek to an unskiied peak then go diving in the frozen ocean. Then we learn about sauna culture and how activity should be fun not work

programme.title: Journey Within S01 E01 - Costa Rica
programme.desc: In this episode, we explore Zunya, an eco-village, and its enchanting blue zone concept. We delve into communal living, uncovering its allure. At La Senda, we experience soul cleansing Temazcal and a sublime cacao ceremony. We engage in a farm-to-table activity, savouring locally sourced ingredients. Cala Luna offers a rejuvenating body treatment amidst serene surroundings. We encounter the Maleku Tribes, witnessing their dance ceremony and visiting their cacao farm. The journey concludes with a breath taking hike and a majestic volcano view, reminding us of nature's beauty. This episode is a transformative adventure of self-discovery, cultural immersion, and connection with nature.

programme.title: Journey Within S01 E02 - Ireland
programme.desc: Marilia's Irish journey, meeting land enthusiasts, explored scenic spots, encounters with Ellie and Hazel, vegan delights at Cornucopia, and concluded with a reflective sunset in the Wicklow Mountains, highlighting a strong community spirit.

programme.title: Chasing The Sun S01 E05 - Brazil
programme.desc: Chasing the Sun, explores Brazil's northeast, uncovering vibrant cultures, fishing traditions, and natural beauty. From Fortaleza to Jericoacoara, discover inspiring stories of resilience, community, and the unique charm of this captivating region.

programme.title: Chasing The Sun S01 E06 - Brazil
programme.desc: Chasing the Sun, explores Brazil's northeast, uncovering vibrant cultures, unique traditions, and natural wonders. From capoeira to the world's largest cashew tree, discover inspiring stories of resilience, community, and the beauty of this captivating region.

programme.title: Departures S03 E08 - Ethiopia - Saints And Snakes
programme.desc: Scott and Justin explore the spiritual side of Ethiopia. In Lalibella, they visit ancient churches carved entirely out of stone, and Justin tries teaching a class of school children some new words. They visit the castles in Gondor and the Church of Saint Mary of Zion, which claims to house the lost Ark of the Covenant. At the Debra Damos monastery, accessible only by climbing a rope up a sheer cliff, they share some difficult but hilarious conversation with a well-spirited monk, who has some poignant things to say about brotherhood and human origins.

programme.title: Departures S03 E09 - Ethiopia - Dances With Bulls
programme.desc: Scott and Justin meet up with Robel, who takes the guys to visit his sisters in his village of Harar. They visit the bustling chat market, and learn how to prepare the traditional stimulant. That night, Robel takes them to feed some wild hyenas by hand. Leaving Harar, they take a long drive to visit the Hammer Tribe. Here they witness a rare ceremony in which the women of the village offer themselves to be whipped, and one soon-to-be married man has to run, stark naked over a dozen bulls, to prove his worth as a husband.

programme.title: Departures S03 E10 - Rwanda
programme.desc: Rwanda is a country emerging from its darkest years of poaching and the 1994 genocide. Scott and Justin travel with locals to learn more about how Rwanda is managing today. They explore the recovery of the animal population – from swimming cows on Napoleon Island to trekking into the wild to visit isolated groups of mountain gorillas. This experience is contrasted with a visit to the church of Ntarama to talk to a genocide survivor, and a court tribunal of several accused men. The guys hear stories of forgiveness from the people, and learn of their hope for their country.

programme.title: Southern Uncovered with the Lee Brothers S01 E03 - Asheville, NC
programme.desc: Matt and Ted Lee forage for wild ramps, throw down some local clay with a renowned ceramic artist and sample an unexpected pairing of yoga and beer in Asheville, North Carolina.

programme.title: Southern Uncovered with the Lee Brothers S01 E04 - Louisville, KY NB
programme.desc: Matt and Ted Lee descend on Louisville, Kentucky, to brew soy sauce in bourbon barrels, explore Mayan-meets-Southern cuisine and get a new taste of the traditional Hot Brown sandwich.

programme.title: Euromaxx À La Carte E74 - From Sky Tables To Cairo Kitchens
programme.desc: The Enduring Success of ‘Dinner in the Sky’ Tasty Lamb Goulash Gheimeh Bademjan From Iran Wine From the Greek Mainland Revolutionary Cuisine: Mikkel Karstad A Dish to Cook at Home: Koshary From Cairo.

programme.title: Euromaxx À La Carte E75 - From Nigeria To Piedmont’s Harvest
programme.desc: Argentinian Empanadas With Beef Filling Meeting of International Gourmet Chefs Prize-Winning Veggies Truffle Season in Piedmont Egusi A Delicious African Dish From Nigeria.

programme.title: Euromaxx À La Carte E76 - Tastes Of Tradition And Trend
programme.desc: Moroccan Cuisine in Berlin Premium Wines From the Moselle Valley Amsterdam’s Avocado Show Restaurant Marvelous Merveilleux! A Piece of Homeland The Taste of Norway.

programme.title: Euromaxx À La Carte E77 - World’s Oldest Restaurant, Pizza, Crab
programme.desc: Crab Soufflé With Cheese – From Chile A Waste-Free Restaurant The World's Oldest Restaurant Deep-Fried Delicacy Tasty Traditions: Neapolitan Pizza Making Filipino Fare in Berlin.

programme.title: Descending S01 E06 - The Deep
programme.desc: Continuing their Caribbean island hopping, the Descending team contemplates underwater art in Grenada, comes face to face with the ocean’s largest carnivore in Dominica, and then goes deeper (1000+ ft!) than they ever have before.

programme.title: Survival Of The Species S01 E01 - Sharks
programme.desc: Ben explores the human impact on sharks, and how they - and we - can be best protected.

programme.title: Travel Guides New Zealand S01 E01 - Northland
programme.desc: Meet the five families chosen to be New Zealand’s Travel Guides as they take on their first adventure in Northland. But there’s a few unhappy campers when the sleeping arrangements are revealed.

programme.title: Travel Man S04 E01 - St. Petersburg
programme.desc: Comedian Rob Beckett joins Richard Ayoade in Russia's cultural capital, for a post-Soviet itinerary including an armoured tank, caviar, vodka, and a herring in a fur coat.

programme.title: Travel Man S04 E02 - Budapest
programme.desc: Richard and comedian Aisling Bea make a whirlwind tour of Hungary's fairy-tale capital, with selfies, goulash, cake, pubs, liquor, and a game of chess at the thermal baths.

programme.title: Travel Man S04 E03 - Tenerife
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade packs his case and heads to the Canaries to discover what lies beyond the all-inclusive resorts of the volcanic island of Tenerife. Making the trip from LA to join him is writer, director and actor, Lena Dunham.

programme.title: Travel Man S04 E04 - Miami
programme.desc: Richard and Rhod Gilbert's high-speed visit to Florida's famous hot spot takes in art, art deco, alligators, speedboats, an airboat tour of the everglades - and dominoes.

programme.title: Race Across The World S03 E05 - Episode 5 NB
programme.desc: The BAFTA-award winning third series of Race Across the World has reached the half-way point and our four teams are one leg closer to the £20,000 cash prize.

programme.title: Burning Pain
programme.desc: After his wife becomes sick and unable to move properly, Michael takes on the extra responsibility of caring for her. Is he doing this out of the goodness of his heart or is there something nefarious going on behind close doors?

programme.title: My Burden
programme.desc: The demise of a couple comes after their daughter gets kidnapped by a sex trafficking ring. They both have different ideas on what to do to bring her home successfully.

programme.title: In Bloom - Aféfé
programme.desc: A collection of 5 short films addressing gender equality issues such as period poverty, child marriage, gender-based violence, HIV and economic empowerment. Fifth Short: "Aféfé," written and directed by Dolapo 'LowlaDee' Adeleke (Nigeria). When her elderly and ailing mother-in-law moves in unexpectedly, life for an impassioned local beautician takes a frightening turn.

programme.title: Pains Of Rhoda
programme.desc: A woman whose husband is sterile goes through a lot of emotional ups and downs as she tries to console him during their trying time but, his trust issues causes more problems instead.

programme.title: The Hangout S5 ep13

programme.title: Car Heist
programme.desc: Two police recruits witness the murder of a foreigner and decide to steal the million-dollar cash left in the victim's car, but their plan keeps on failing.

programme.title: Sisi Eko - Ep12
programme.desc: Eko is beautiful and knows it, so it is no surprise that she gets most of the male attention. Finding Mr Right, however, is proving to be complicated, as Mr Right must meet her '6-Cardinal Rules'. The romantic comedy series touches on important issues such as mental health, anxiety, and self-doubt.

programme.title: Sisi Eko - Ep 13
programme.desc: Eko is beautiful and knows it, so it is no surprise that she gets most of the male attention. programme.title: Make A Move
programme.desc: An ex-convict gets pulled back into her old ways when she takes on a job that leads to the murder of an innocent man. Things get further complicated when she finds herself involved with his son who is out seeking revenge.

programme.title: The Drive
programme.desc: Becky and Chude are excited for their growing family, not realising just how much their unsavoury lifestyle will impact them.

programme.title: The Hangout S5 Ep4
programme.desc: The Hangout returns for its fifth season with a group of opinionated, social media-savvy millennials who debate controversial topics on love, sex, friendship, and more.

programme.title: Imperfection
programme.desc: A man struggles to deal with a nagging wife while she also finds difficulty keeping up with his nonchalance. But a balance must be struck if they'll make marriage a success.

programme.title: Sade
programme.desc: AMCOP Exclusive Movie. A teenage girl fails to return home after a day of hawking oranges and the local hoodlums are the first suspects for her disappearance, however the investigation shows that all is not as it seems.

programme.title: AMC Music Lounge S7 - Maio
programme.desc: AMC Music Lounge returns for its seventh season bringing you some new interviews, new live performances, fun musical games, and many more from legendary and emerging artistes.

programme.title: Wrongfully Hers
programme.desc: Hijinks follows after Perpetual, pretending to be a sophisticated socialite, befriends Gina who has fallen in love with Esa, not realising he is Perpetual's unappreciated boyfriend.

programme.title: Check-Mate
programme.desc: Jada, suspicious about her husband's reason for always being tired, decides to hire an escort to test his fidelity. Things don't go according to plan, and she puts their relationship into jeopardy.

programme.title: Grim
programme.desc: AMCOP Exclusive Movie. Charles loses his job shortly after settling down with his wife, Abigail. Things become pretty difficult for the family as Abigail is a fresh graduate and yet to secure a job, as a result, she cannot not help financially. They decide to hold off on having children, however, Abigail gets pregnant and they cannot agree on how to handle it. The situations not helped by the unexpected arrival of Charles' mother, Evelyn.

programme.title: Hunt
programme.desc: The CEO of a trucking company is acquitted during a high profile human trafficking case but when he wants to go legitimate, his shady business partner is determined to do everything in his power to keep their arrangement going.

programme.title: 31 Days
programme.desc: Two young corpers find themselves in the unknown when they are assigned to a small village for their youth service and try to teach new ways of doing things that defy the village's traditions.

programme.title: AMC Red Carpet - Banke Naya The Homecoming
programme.desc: AMC Red Carpet moments brings you exclusive red carpet interviews with Nollywood stars at the premieres of top Nollywood movies and other industry events such as award ceremonies.

programme.title: Cast On A Stone
programme.desc: Lisa’s life becomes a life lesson for an irresponsible father, after her criminal past catches up with her when her ex-boyfriend and current husband cross paths.

programme.title: Lurk
programme.desc: A woman dedicated to her work is unaware that she has made enemies who would stop at nothing to see her dead.

programme.title: The Missing Package
programme.desc: In The Missing Package, Detective Lazarus is almost done for the day when an unassuming middle-aged man walks in and reports a package he is expecting missing, but more importantly is the carrier who seems to have disappeared into thin air. This sets Detective Lazarus on a wild goose chase to find what appears to be a package determined not to be found and a carrier who appears to no longer exist.

programme.title: Scars Ep 23
programme.desc: Rubby is a singer who has a promising future in the world of music. Unfortunately, her biggest secret may be discovered when a journalist comes sniffing around. Behind that bombshell lies stories of trafficking, abuse, scams, and abandonment.

programme.desc: Rubby is a singer who has a promising future in the world of music. Unfortunately, her biggest secret may be discovered when a journalist comes sniffing around. Behind that bombshell lies stories of trafficking, abuse, scams, and abandonment.

programme.title: Aces Of Hurt
programme.desc: AMCOP Exclusive Movie. Displeased with his son's choice of a wife, a deadbeat father is out to show his son’s fiancée hell over the course of the weekend, but this fire he is stoking will uncover a truth that might be the bane of the both of them.

programme.title: Frenemy
programme.desc: In the process of divorcing her husband, Peggy moves out of the house to start a new life and, hopefully, to forget all about the opposite gender, but life has other plans for her.

programme.title: Shaffy's Sister
programme.desc: Shaffy’s sister, Daisy, struggles to interact with her family and find her place in society after spending years in prison.

programme.title: The Keeper
programme.desc: Armed robbers strike a young man desperate not to do away with his boss’ 50,000 dollars—what he considers a ticket to his elusive good side.

programme.title: Pressured
programme.desc: A woman is regarded as a spinster after a series of failed romances and feeling pressure from all around her to settle down with the next man that comes along.

programme.title: Hell Mary
programme.desc: Mary, set to takeover her mother’s fashion empire, is used to getting her way. Taking an interest in Andre, the chief photographer, she decides to start work in her mother’s company. She becomes obsessed with him and does everything in her power to get his attention. Things take a diabolical turn when Andre constantly rebuffs her advances.

programme.title: The Wrap
programme.desc: The Wrap is a nightly recap show, delivering the day’s top market headlines and most insightful interviews from Schwab Network's daily market coverage. Airing daily at 7pm ET, it highlights key takeaways from the trading session, expert analysis, and actionable ideas from the network’s best conversations—giving investors a concise, informative look at what moved the markets and what to watch next.

programme.title: Morning Movers
programme.desc: Don’t miss the go-to resource for pre-market action from the floor of the New York Stock Exchange. Join Diane King Hall for breaking economic news, expert analysis, technical insights, and real-time trading strategies.

programme.title: Opening Bell
programme.desc: Opening Bell

programme.title: Morning Trade Live
programme.desc: Sam Vadas takes traders beyond the headlines with a sharp focus on the macro drivers, sector rotations, and global forces shaping the day’s market action. From breaking down U.S. and international economic data to analyzing sector performance and cross-asset moves, Morning Trade Live delivers the context traders need to understand the “why” behind early trading activity.

programme.title: Trading 360
programme.desc: After the dust settles from the opening bell, Trading 360 with Marley Kayden features Wall Street’s best traders, analysts and technicians to break down sectors and stocks moving markets. Trading 360 focuses on disruptive companies and new technologies driving market headlines while providing investors a 360-degree actionable approach to trading.

programme.title: Fast Market
programme.desc: Host Diane King Hall teams up with veteran traders Tom White and Kevin Hincks to break down live market moves and deliver actionable trade ideas in real time. From options strategies and volatility plays to earnings reactions, they bring in market specialists, analysts, and guests to give active traders the setups and strategies they can use right now.

programme.title: Next Gen Investing
programme.desc: The future of trading is here on Next Gen Investing. Alex Coffey and Jenny Horne take viewers through the markets from a unique perspective to help new investors pursue their financial goals. Next Gen Investing will explore various trading strategies while highlighting trending names and under the radar stocks in the market, while walking investors through real-time example trades.

programme.title: The Watch List
programme.desc: The Watch List with Nicole Petallides offers investors a midday look at the most relevant stocks, sectors and commodities. Petallides hosts a panel of experts ranging from industry-leading CEOs, analysts and Wall Street influencers to discuss pressing topics moving the market in real-time. The Watch List provides viewers with a midpoint status update for the end of the trading day and beyond.

programme.title: Market On Close

Gewandhausorchester, Andris Nelsons & Baiba Skride - Nelsons conducts Tchaikovsky and Shostakovich
programme.desc: In May 2019, the new principal conductor of the Gewandhaus, Andris Nelsons, presented Tchaikovsky’s Symphony No. 5 with his orchestra in combination with Shostakovich’s Violin Concerto No. 1 with outstanding violinist Baiba Skride as the soloist. In this composition, the violin does not allow itself a break, continually tells its dark story and gets into a vicious circle of ostinato Passacaglia bass lines again and again falls into beguilingly beautiful singing. Tchaikovsky initially thought his 'Symphony of Fate' was a failure and believed himself to be at the end of his creative powers. It was probably the composer's nature, plagued by self-doubt, that made it almost impossible for him to develop a self-confident attitude to his own creative power. Between this two works, Skride performs Stravinsky's Elegy for Solo Violin.

programme.title: Philippe Jordan, Orchestre de l’Opéra national de Paris, Choeur de l’Opéra national de Paris & Ricarda Merbeth - Beethoven - Symphony No. 9 in D minor, Op. 125
programme.desc: Swiss conductor Philippe Jordan and the Orchestre de l’Opéra national de Paris recorded all of Ludwig van Beethoven’s symphonies in 2014-2015. In this program, maestro Jordan presents Beethoven’s final symphony: Symphony No. 9 in D minor, Op. 125. He conducts the Orchestre and Choeurs de l’Opéra national de Paris and four vocal soloists Ricarda Merbeth (soprano), Daniela Sindram (mezzosoprano), Robert Dean Smith (tenor), and Günther Groissböck (bass). Beethoven composed his last Symphony when his hearing had all but gone, between 1822 and 1824. Symphony No. 9 is the longest and most ambitious of Beethoven’s symphonies. Most striking is the piece’s finale movement, which includes a choir and four vocal soloists singing a setting of Friedrich Schiller’s poem ‘An die Freude’ (Ode to Joy). This performance was recorded at Opéra Bastille in Paris, France, in 2015.

programme.title: Leonard Slatkin & Orchestre National de Lyon - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Gabriela Montero, Youth Orchestra of the Americas & Carlos Miguel Prieto - Gabriela Montero plays Ravel & Montero
programme.desc: Venezuelan pianist Gabriela Montero is accompanied by the Youth Orchestra of the Americas conducted by Carlos Miguel Prieto. On the program are Maurice Ravel’s Piano Concerto in G Major, as well as two of Montero’s own works: the “Latin Concerto” and Improvisations. The performance took place in one of the world's most beautiful concert halls, the Teatro del Lago in Frutillar, located in the middle of the Patagonian plains. The concert hall, which opened its doors in 2010, stands on the banks of Chile's second-largest lake overlooking a volcano that is capped with snow the entire year. In an accompanying interview, Montero speaks about composing, improvising, and performing works of her own alongside those of others. She discusses her home country of Venezuela, where she no longer performs for political reasons.

programme.title: Utrecht String Quartet - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ballets de Monte Carlo - La Belle
programme.desc: 'La Belle' (The Sleeping Beauty) is a three-act ballet by award-winning French choreographer Jean-Christophe Maillot. In 1993, Maillot became artistic director of the Ballets de Monte-Carlo, creating over 30 ballets for the company, including 'Romeo and Juliet' (1996), 'Scheherazade' (2009), and 'Opus 50' (2011). Based on Charles Perrault’s famous fairy tale, Maillot staged this unconventional and unique version of 'La Belle', which explores the darker side of Perrault’s story. The incredible choreography is set to the score of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky, performed by the Monte-Carlo Philharmonic Orchestra under the baton of Nicolas Brochot. Besides the performers of the Ballets de Monte-Carlo, the production features star dancers of the Bolshoi Ballet: Semyon Chudin (Prince) and Olga Smirnova (La Belle). This performance was recorded at the Salle des Princes of the Grimaldi Forum in Monaco, in December 2016.

programme.title: Alessandro Marangoni - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Gewandhausorchester, Andris Nelsons & Baiba Skride - Nelsons conducts Tchaikovsky and Shostakovich
programme.desc: In May 2019, the new principal conductor of the Gewandhaus, Andris Nelsons, presented Tchaikovsky’s Symphony No. 5 with his orchestra in combination with Shostakovich’s Violin Concerto No. 1 with outstanding violinist Baiba Skride as the soloist. In this composition, the violin does not allow itself a break, continually tells its dark story and gets into a vicious circle of ostinato Passacaglia bass lines again and again falls into beguilingly beautiful singing. Tchaikovsky initially thought his 'Symphony of Fate' was a failure and believed himself to be at the end of his creative powers. It was probably the composer's nature, plagued by self-doubt, that made it almost impossible for him to develop a self-confident attitude to his own creative power. Between this two works, Skride performs Stravinsky's Elegy for Solo Violin.

programme.title: Orquesta Sinfónica de Minería & José Areán - Mahler - Symphony No. 10
programme.desc: Mahler's Symphony No. 10 is an unfinished work left by the composer at his death, representing his ultimate emotional journey from torment and heartbreak to an unreserved acceptance of life and human love. The symphony, built on the composer's personal anguish and existential explorations, is characterized by its deep emotional range, intricate orchestration, and epic scale, culminating in a powerful affirmation of human connection in the face of mortality. Deryck Cook completed a version of the work with the help of materials provided by Mahler's widow.

programme.title: Martha Argerich - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Thomas Dunford - Works for lute by J. S. Bach
programme.desc: French lutenist Thomas Dunford performs various works for lute by J. S. Bach in this intimate chamber music concert, recorded at Concertgebouw Brugge, Belgium, in 2017. On the program are Suite for lute in G minor, BWV 995; Suite for solo cello in G major (arr. for lute), BWV 1007; and Chaconne from Partita for solo violin in D minor (arr. lute), BWV 1004. Paris-born lutenist Thomas Dunford (*1988) discovered the lute at the age of nine. He studied at the Conservatory of Paris and the Schola Cantorum in Basel, and participated in masterclasses with lutenists such as Rolf Lislevand and Julian Bream. He has made various solo and ensemble appearances at Europe’s most prestigious festivals.

programme.title: Marin Alsop - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Ballets de Monte Carlo - Romeo and Juliet
programme.desc: Sergei Prokofiev based his ballet ‘Romeo and Juliet’ on William Shakespeare's famous play of the same name, telling the story of two young lovers from the feuding families Montague and Capulet. Acclaimed French choreographer Jean-Christophe Maillot created a distinctive choreography of the ballet in 1996, which has been performed to great success the world over. In his production, Maillot interprets Romeo and Juliet not as a social conflict or clan warfare governed by a strict code of honor, but as a tale of accidental tragedy that leads to the death of children more concerned with the path of love than that of hatred. This 2002 interpretation is performed by the dancers of Les Ballets de Monte Carlo. Chris Roelandt and Bernice Coppieters star in the title roles.

programme.title: Thomas Triesschijn - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Pavel Milyukov & Boris Berezovsky - Works for violin by Franck, Fauré, Schubert a.o.
programme.desc: Russian violinist Pavel Milyukov and pianist Boris Berezovsky join forces in this concert recorded at Tchaikovsky Concert Hall in Moscow, Russia, on October 15, 2020. Milyukov has won prizes at various international competitions and has appeared at venues throughout Europe. He forms an excellent chamber music duo with acclaimed pianist Boris Berezovsky. On this concert’s program are César Franck’s Sonata for violin and piano; Gabriel Fauré’s Après un rêve; Alfred Schnittke’s Stille Nacht; and Franz Schubert’s Erlkönig. As an encore, the duo performs ‘Russian Song’ from Igor Stravinsky’s opera Mavra (arranged by S. Dushkin).

programme.title: Gwendolyn Masin - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Sir Simon Rattle & London Symphony Orchestra - The LSO performs Dvořák and Tchaikovsky
programme.desc: Sir Simon Rattle conducts the London Symphony Orchestra (LSO) in a concert program consisting of Antonín Dvořák’s Scherzo Capriccioso in D-flat major, Op. 66, and selections from Act II of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky’s famous ballet The Nutcracker (1892). Dvořák’s Scherzo Capriccioso (1883) reflects a period of personal crisis for the composer. The work betrays ever-changing moods and a constant sense of inner restlessness. In the music of The Nutcracker, Tchaikovsky combined memorable melodies with colorful orchestration, which has enchanted listeners for decades. This performance was recorded at LSO St Luke’s in London, UK, on March 18, 2021.

programme.title: Kristof Barati - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Mariss Jansons, Berliner Philharmoniker & Hilary Hahn - The Berlin Philharmonic in Tokyo
programme.desc: The Berliner Philharmoniker is one of the world’s finest orchestras, its fame stretching all the way to Japan. Unsurprisingly, this 2000 concert, recorded at Tokyo’s magnificent Suntory Hall, was a resounding success. Mariss Jansons conducts a beautiful program that includes Antonin Dvorák’s Symphony No. 8, Carl Maria von Weber’s Overture to Oberon, and Dmitri Shostakovich’s Violin Concerto featuring star violinist Hilary Hahn (*1979)

programme.title: Martha Argerich - Classica Spotlight
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Michael Mossman - Munich Philharmonic Hall: Michel Camilo Quintet
programme.desc: Well-known for his exuberant style and intimate familiarity with a vast range of jazz and Latin idioms, Michel Camilo has always had a sixth sense for musical adventure. Camilo was one of the most stimulating jazz pianists to emerge in the mid- to late '80s. His powerful two-handed attack (full of impressive technical skills), his knowledge of both jazz and Afro-Cuban music, and his willingness to constantly take chances turn each of his solos into an adventure. This broadcast was taped during the 1991 Münchner Klaviersommer, when Camilo was playing with his amazing Quintet. He is accompanied by Ned Mann (bass), Cliff Almond (drums), Michel Mossman (trumpet and trombone) and Ralph Bowen (saxophone and flute). Camilo's rise to fame has been swift, and his influences range from jazz musicians like Art Tatum and Keith Jarrett to twentieth-century composers like Debussy and Ravel.

programme.title: Ted Curson - DJAZZ Clips
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Jazzmeia Horn - He's My Guy
programme.desc: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn’s 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance was a celebration of jazz’s future, infused with the soul of its past. Featuring Keith Brown (piano), Rashaan Carter (bass), Irwin Hall (flute and saxophone), and Anwar Marshall (drums), the young singer’s performance was a reminder of jazz’s enduring power to speak to the heart. Situated at the Valletta waterfront outside Our Lady of Liesse Church, Horn’s concert was a truly spellbinding experience.

programme.title: Jimmy Witherspoon & Rozaa Wortham - Jimmy Witherspoon Quartet & Rozaa Wortham 1985
programme.desc: Since 1977, Estival is a summer jazz festival in Switzerland, Lugano. Estival offers a thrilling and particularly surprising line-up that explores the rich world of contemporary music whilst promoting the understanding of different cultures, tolerance, and co-existence. Jimmy Witherspoon and Rozaa Wortham, two incredible brilliant jazz musicians. Rozaa Wortham starts off the show with the Jimmy Witherspoon Quartet. Swinging and full of energy she performs ‘It Don’t Mean A Thing’. Jimmy Witherspoon, known for the song “Ain’t Nobody’s Business” ends the show with some astounding lean-back blues.

programme.title: Kenny Barron - Kenny Barron, Song for Abdullah
programme.desc: Pianist Kenny Barron’s June 27, 2019 appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine’s Lviv was a celebration of sublime jazz virtuosity. The revered elder statesmen of jazz piano brought his regular working trio of Kiyoshi Kitagawa (bass) and Jonathan Blake (drums), augmented by stellar jazzman Marcus Strickland (tenor saxophone) and young lion Riley Mulherkar (trumpet). Their seamless blend of classic bebop and modern explorations spoke of a deep, emotive connection to each note, captivating the Ukrainian audience with lyrical improvisations during this unforgettable concert.

programme.title: Le Quatuor Ebène - Jacques Loussier in Leipzig: Play Bach and More
programme.desc: In 1959, Jacques Loussier hit upon the idea that was to make his international reputation, by combining his interest in jazz with his love of J.S. Bach. Only a pianist with such an exceptional classical technique and deft improvisatory skill could have nurtured such a vision. He founded the Play Bach Trio, which used Bach's compositions as the basis for jazz improvisation. The trio immediately caught the public imagination. In their live appearances, tours and concerts, plus a succession of recordings built on the cornerstone of four albums made between 1960 and 1963, Loussier's group achieved the breakthrough to popular commercial success enjoyed by only a select few jazz musicians. In fifteen years, the trio sold over six million albums! On July 28th 2004, the day of Bach's 254th anniversary of death, the Jacques Loussier Trio perform a special concert at the famous St. Thomas Church of Leipzig - the city where Bach composed many of his most famous works.

programme.title: Jaco Pastorius - Fannie Mae
programme.desc: The audience at the Montreal Jazz Festival in Canada was in for a treat on July 3, 1982, as a true musical innovator hit the stage: Jaco Pastorius, who transformed the electric bass into a formidable solo instrument. Combining intricate harmonics, fluid melodies, and unparalleled technical skill, Pastorius left a lasting impact on the music world. In this concert, he is accompanied by Peter Erskine (drums), Don Alias (percussion), Othello Molineaux (steel drums), Bob Mintzer (tenor sax and bass clarinet), and Randy Brecker (trumpet and electronics). They perform “Chicken”, “Donna Lee”, “Mr. Phone Bone”, and “Fannie Mae”. Don't miss this extraordinary opportunity to experience Jaco Pastorius’s electrifying performance at the Montreal Jazz Festival 1982!

programme.title: Chucho Valdés, Concha Buika, Rodney Barreto & Dreiser Durruthy Bombale - Chucho Valdes - Jazz a Vienne
programme.desc: ‘Jazz a Vienne’ is one of the world's most prestigious jazz festivals. Ever since 1981 it has attracted leading jazz artists, with many of them playing return engagements. Unsurprisingly, the festival's 2013 edition did not fall short of expectations: the lineup was filled with the biggest names in music. Among them were Chucho Valdes and his Afro-Cuban Messengers, the prime example of Latin-inspired jazz music. Together with vocalists Concha Buika and Dreiser Durruthy Bombale, Rodney Barreto on drums, Gaston Joya on double bass, Yaroldy Abreu Robles on congas, and Reinaldo Melian Alvarez on trumpet, the legendary pianist brings the streets of Havana to the festival’s stage.

programme.title: Billie Holiday - Fine and Mellow
programme.desc: “God Bless The Child”, “Foolin’ Myself”, and “I Loves You, Porgy”: timeless songs that were made classics by the inimitable Billie Holiday. ‘Lady Day’, as her loyal friend and musical partner Lester Young nicknamed her, was a highly original jazz and pop singer. In a career that spanned most of the 1930s, 1940s and 1950s, she displayed an uncanny ability to convey emotion through her voice. Her vocals, which were strongly inspired by instrumental jazz, are admired for their deeply personal and intimate sound. This compilation features recordings from the 1950s of Billie Holiday singing a string of her greatest successes. Enjoy this unique footage of one of jazz’s all-time greats!

programme.title: Les McCann - Les McCann: Live in New Orleans
programme.desc: Self-taught musician Les McCann became the international jazz superstar he is today after the release of his album “Swiss Movement” which he recorded in 1968 with the late Eddie Harris. Yet there is much more to this musician than that one record. McCann moves comfortably from one jazz style to the next, demonstrating impressive chops in all areas, from bop to fusion, and from vocals to the keys of the electric piano, clavinet, or synthesizer. His mix of church and swing music captures the spirit of the time perfectly, even when an illness prevented him from playing with more than one finger at a time in the early 1990s. In today’s broadcast, McCann takes gospel back to New Orleans, where he played this set in 1983. McCann’s vocals shine in the soulful performances of several of his hits, including “Just Like Magic”, backed by his wonderful “Magic Band” of saxophonist Bobby Bryant Jr., bassist Curtis Robertson Jr., and drummer Tony St. James.

programme.title: Tom Excell, Pilo Adami, Nick Richards & Finn Booth - Nubiyan Twist - Music Meeting
programme.desc: Since 1985, the Dutch city of Nijmegen has been welcoming the International Music Meeting Festival every Pentecost. Ranging from jazz and improvisational music to traditional music and unique crossovers, this adventurous festival is all about musical encounters and international collaborations. In 2019, the band Nubiyan Twist performs at the International Music Meeting Festival. This London-based group operates on the intersection of soul and jazz.

programme.title: Ted Curson - DJAZZ Clips
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Benny Goodman, Scott Hamilton, John Bunch & Phil Flanigan - Benny Goodman Septet - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival’s staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1982, legendary jazz clarinettist Benny Goodman performed at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague with his septet. The “King of Swing” revisited the atmosphere of the 1930s, when jazz enjoyed tremendous popularity.

programme.title: Kenny Barron - Kenny Barron, Blue Waters
programme.desc: Pianist Kenny Barron’s June 27, 2019 appearance at the Alfa Jazz Festival in Ukraine’s Lviv was a celebration of sublime jazz virtuosity. The revered elder statesmen of jazz piano brought his regular working trio of Kiyoshi Kitagawa (bass) and Jonathan Blake (drums), augmented by stellar jazzman Marcus Strickland (tenor saxophone) and young lion Riley Mulherkar (trumpet). Their seamless blend of classic bebop and modern explorations spoke of a deep, emotive connection to each note, captivating the Ukrainian audience with lyrical improvisations during this unforgettable concert.

programme.title: Coleman Hawkins - DJAZZ Clips
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Walter Trout, Johnny Griparic, Teddy Andreadis "Zig Zag" & Michael Leasure - Walter Trout Band - Jazzwoche Burghausen
programme.desc: Every year since 1970, the German city of Burghausen has been hosting one of the largest jazz festivals in the world. During Burghausen International Jazz Week, Burghausen becomes a 'Bavarian jazz mecca': guests from all over the world join the Burghausers to enjoy the most wonderful jazz performances as the colorful hustle and bustle of spectators and musicians shake up the city. One of the bands performing here in 2019 is the Walter Trout Band. Trout, who was born in New Jersey and is based in Los Angeles, is a world-famous contemporary blues rock guitarist and vocalist.

programme.title: Avishai Cohen - Avishai Cohen/Tscho Theissing, Arab Medley
programme.desc: Double bassist Avishai Cohen’s trio with Noam David (drums) and Omri Mor (pianist) joined forces with the international Symphony Orchestra ‘INSO-Lviv’, conducted by Christian Schumann. On June 24, 2017 they brought Avishai Cohen’s signature blend of influences from Eastern Europe, American jazz and the Middle East to the Alfa Jazz Festival in Lviv, Ukraine. Cohen, who also supplies vocalizations in the Judeo-Spanish dialect Ladino, came to prominence internationally in the 1990s when jazz great Chick Corea offered him a place in his trio and a recording deal. Thirty years later, Cohen has become a house-hold name himself, leaving his Ukrainian audience enthralled and connected through the universal language of music.

programme.title: Fats Domino - Fats Domino - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival’s staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. American singer and pianist Fats Domino gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival of 1980. Domino was one of the pioneers of rock and roll music, selling more than 65 million records and having 11 top hits between 1955 and 1960.

programme.title: Woody Herman - DJAZZ Clips
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: The Kenny Garrett Quintet - The Kenny Garrett Quintet
programme.desc: Appearing in the early 1980s at a time when some of jazz was refocusing on some of its historic values, Kenny Garrett was the first to show, through the dramatic intensity of his playing, that the alto could once again compete with the tenor saxophone, the instrument par excellence of the quest for jazz since John Coltrane, a musician whose influence he loudly and clearly claims. "Sounds from the Ancestors" his latest album, reflects the rich history of jazz, R&B and gospel music from the saxophonist's hometown of Detroit. But more importantly, it also has a modern cosmopolitan vibe, including music from France, Cuba, Nigeria and Guadeloupe.

programme.title: Chucho Valdis - Ochun Chucho
programme.desc: Legendary Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brought his band ‘Jazz Batá 2’ to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019. The air was thick with anticipation as Valdés took to the stage, revisiting the small-group concept of his mythical 1972 Cuban album ‘Jazz Batá’. The batá repertoire – the deep classical music of West Africa – permeates Valdés’ piano solos throughout the concert. Valdés’s band consists of Dreiser Durruthy (Batas and vocals), Yaroldy Abreu (percussions and vocals), Abraham Mansfarroll (percussion), Ramón Vazquez Martirena-Bajo (bass guitar). They delivered a performance that reverberated through the historic island.

programme.title: Lianne La Havas - Green & Gold
programme.desc: Lianne La Havas’ performance at Casino de Paris captures the British singer-songwriter at her best: with her crystalline voice she performs soulful melodies full of sincerity. The performance is part of La Havas’ 'Blood Tour', a concert tour promoting her second album 'Blood' (2015). She is accompanied by James Wyatt on keys, Adam Prendergast on bass, Giles King Ashong on drums, and Frida Touray on backing vocals.

programme.title: James Brown - James Brown - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival’s staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. Back in 1981, legendary American singer James Brown gave an unforgettable performance at the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague. The "Godfather of Soul", "Mr. Dynamite", and "Soul Brother No. 1" was a major force in 20th century popular music, influencing many musicians in numerous genres.

programme.title: Eliane Elias - Münchner Klaviersommer: Eliane Elias Trio
programme.desc: There’s no question about it: with a Grammy for Best Latin Jazz Album for her recording ‘Made In Brazil’, seven previous Grammy nominations, four ‘Gold Disc Awards’, and three ‘Best Vocal Album Awards’ in Japan, Eliane Elias has taken her place in the pantheon of music giants. In her projects, Elias wears many hats: she serves as producer, composer, lyricist, arranger, pianist, and vocalist. In this concert broadcast, recorded live at the Philharmonie in Munich, Elias shows her distinctive and immediately recognizable musical style, which blends her Brazilian samba roots and her sensuous, alluring voice with her impressive instrumental bebop, jazz, classical, and compositional skills. She is an artist beyond category, and in this concert she performs with Jay Randall Anderson (bass) and Adam Nussbaum (drums).

programme.title: Dizzy Gillespie, Tony Coe, Derek Humble & Billy Mitchell - Things Are Here
programme.desc: American jazz trumpeter, composer, and bandleader Dizzy Gillespie (1917-1993) was one of the seminal figures of the bebop movement. He fuses all musical forms rooted in African culture, such as music from Cuba, Latin America and the Caribbean, into his music. On November 4, 1970 he played a concert in Denmark with the Kenny Clarke/Francy Boland Big Band, performing Con Alma, Brother K, Now Hear My Meanin’, Manteca, Let Me Outta Here, and Things Are Here.

programme.title: A Day in Paradise 2
programme.desc: A graciously flowing four hour long pure nature relaxation experience, "A Day In Paradise" immerses viewers in the sights and sounds of one of the world's most pure and picturesque tropical islands - for an entire day from sunrise, to sunset. Filmed in 2013 by David Huting, the tiny island is hidden off of the northeastern coast of the outer Fiji Islands (near Taveuni), and boasts a perfect white sandy beach, groves of palm trees, and a majestic lagoon that shelters the island from all sides. It has more turtle visitors than humans!

programme.title: Superbloom Soaring
programme.desc: Soar over one of the most spectacular and unique spectacles of nature - a spring "superbloom" - for one full hour of pure relaxation bliss. Paired with new soothing music by Relax Moods, this film sweeps viewers on a vibrant, gentle flight over the spring poppy fields of California's Antelope Valley and Lake Elsinore wilderness, as witnessed in 2020 & 2019.

programme.title: South Australia Seas
programme.desc: "South Australian Seas" transports viewers to the remote and gorgeous southern coasts of Australia as captured from above. For one full hour, you'll be gently transported from one vibrant ocean scene to the next paired with real-life ocean sounds and sublimely beautiful ambient music by Relax Moods.

programme.title: Cadlao Lagoon: Philippines (Music)
programme.desc: Experience the stunning beauty of a turquoise lagoon flanked by impressive, vegetation-covered mountains.

programme.title: Shark Paradise
programme.desc: Leave your worries from the world above behind and dive into a vibrant undersea world inhabited by the iconic sharks, for 80 minutes of Nature Relaxation. Captured by filmmaker collaborator Peter Schneider, Shark Paradise features hundreds of unique real-time scenes captured from around the world, paired with gentle underwater sounds & calming music for deep relaxation by Relax Moods.

programme.title: White Beach: Philippines
programme.desc: Laze the day away on a white sand beach in the Philippines as the sun gently sets on the horizon.

programme.title: Return to Paradise 1
programme.desc: Escape to one of the world's most idyllic tropical islands for 2 hours of relaxing on the beach with "Return to Paradise,." The sequel to "A Day in Paradise" (2014), the film returns viewers to the hidden exotic Fijian island of Nanuku - a tiny palm-covered sandy atoll surrounded by magical lagoons brimming with sea life. The film lasts for 2 full hours and features a dynamic mix of stunning tropical island beach & palm tree scenes that will gently transport viewers around the island. Each scene lasts several minutes, allowing time to soak up the views & absorb the healing energies which paradise provides! There are also some aerial drone clips to give the viewer perspective from above.

programme.title: Flying Over South Korea
programme.desc: Experience the brilliance of South Korea's natural wonders & amazing cityscapes with "Flying Over South Korea", an epic 60 minute ambient aerial film. During the film, viewers will serenely adventure to four of the most famous destinations in South Korea: Jeju Island, Seoul City, Geoje National Park, and Busan.

programme.title: Redwood Forest Rejuvenation
programme.desc: Transport yourself to the magical redwood forests of Northern California and soak up the healing energies that only pure Nature can provide. Viewers will enjoy multiple healing scenes from the old-grove forest, paired with real-life stereo nature sounds. Completely remastered from the ground up in August 2020, "Redwood Relaxation" was originally filmed in 2008-2011 by David Huting at Muir Woods National Monument, just north of San Francisco.

programme.title: Towering Redwood Trees, California
programme.desc: Wander beneath the dense canopy of the redwood forest, beneath some of the tallest trees in the world.

programme.title: Wairere Falls: New Zealand (Music)
programme.desc: Discover the breathtaking beauty of a majestic 153-metre waterfall plunging spectacularly over a cliff and onto the boulders below.

programme.title: Chemin Pealey (Music)
programme.desc: Enjoy the view from rocky cliffs that overlook calm waters.

programme.title: Boardwalk to the beach: Guadeloupe
programme.desc: Take a stroll on the boardwalk to the turquoise waters, in Le Gosier, Guadeloupe.

programme.title: Hamilton Island Palms
programme.desc: Relax and soak up the tropical breeze with this stunning palm & coastal vista from the famous Hamilton Island, Australia. Sunlight flickers through the fronds and the waves roll in onto the distant beach, make for an ultra relaxing static scene complete with stereo nature sounds.

programme.title: Santa Ynez Valley: California
programme.desc: Take a walk in the idyllic valley and enjoy calming sights of mature trees, and endless skies.

programme.title: Ruby Beach Sunset
programme.desc: Relax for a full hour as a golden, soothing sunset slowly unfolds and turns to dusk as seen from Ruby Beach, on Washington state's Olympic Peninsula. You'll be lulled into a state of tranquil relaxation thanks to the sounds of rhythmic ocean waves gently crashing over the pebbly beach paired with the dazzling collage of 4 different sunset scenes shot in 4K.

programme.title: Mountains of Majesty
programme.desc: Let the wondrous sights and sounds of the Rocky Mountains fill your soul with peace and inspiration in this ultra-relaxing, full one hour long visual tour of one of Nature's most glorious places. Most of the scenes you'll see here were captured in the Canadian Rockies: Banff, Jasper, and Yoho National Parks, as well as some scenes from Glacier National Park, MT. Enjoy raging waterfalls, crystal clear mountain lakes, snowy mountain-tops, abundant wildlife, colorful wildflowers, and much more as you lose your worries in place of wonders. With just nature sounds.

programme.title: Underwater Wonders
programme.desc: Sit back, relax, and embark on a colorful sea undersea voyage filled with the awe-inspiring wildlife wonders of the coral reefs from around the world. Paired with brand new music by Relax Moods, this 4K version is 1 hour long, making it perfect to leave on as a soothing backdrop during your day or while falling asleep.

programme.desc: Epuyén is known for its beautiful lake of glacier origin, ecotourism activities such as hiking and aquatic sports, and the production of fine fruit and crafts

programme.title: Viajar por mi país - Cafayate, a unique place in Salta
programme.desc: Cafayate, Salta - Perito Moreno Glacier, Santa Cruz - Colón, Entre Ríos - City Bell, Buenos Aires

programme.title: Shapeshifter
programme.desc: A maximum-security prison goes into serious lockdown mode when a shape-shifting demon with an insatiable taste for human flesh unleashes its fury on the inmates and guards.

programme.title: Sennentuntschi
programme.desc: 1975, Swiss Alps: In a remote mountain village, a beautiful and mysterious woman shows up. Only the village policeman takes care of the strange woman and tries to find out who she is. There are hints that she came from the H??henalp Alp, where herdsmen do unthinkable things to get the company of women. Many dark truths are revealed that should better have remained hidden. A tragedy of lust, insanity and murder is brought to a seemlingly pure and perfect world. It is the beginning of a nightmare of religious insanity, hypocrisy, abuse and belief in demons, who destroys a young family and brings a whole village to destruction.

programme.title: Psychos in Love
programme.desc: A strip-joint owner and a manicurist find that they have many things in common, the foremost being that they are psychotic serial killers. They fall in love and are happy being the family that slays together, until one day they come up against a plumber who also happens to be a cannibal.

programme.title: The Coed and the Zombie Stoner
programme.desc: When a sorority girl falls in love with a zombie, it's only a matter of time before a zombie apocalypse is unleashed on campus. The sorority girl discovers that weed is the cure. Now, she must smoke out the whole school before it's too late.

programme.title: Dracula in Love
programme.desc: Leila, a lonely young soul spends the night partying with her friends Nancy, David and Nash in a warehouse. As the boys drink up and smoke pot all social barriers between the sexes crumble. Leila, hit on by both men, hides away from them. Alone she senses there is someone else who wants her and certainly he is not of this world. A 200 year old Dracula escapes from a crate and reveals himself to Leila and tells her that she is his lost love. For him to live again and be with her, she must bring him the blood of her friends to drink and make love to him. Hypnotized by his sexual powers, Leila does as she is told. Leila and a handsome young Dracula get away and live happily ever after.

programme.desc: A maximum-security prison goes into serious lockdown mode when a shape-shifting demon with an insatiable taste for human flesh unleashes its fury on the inmates and guards.
